bio: Joyce Kathryn Roorda, later Joyce Kathryn Lourens, was born in Pella and grew up attending Pleasant Grove and Maple Grove country schools before graduating from Prairie City High School in 1957. After high school she worked for the State Health Department, then focused on raising her sons, Douglas and Kevin. In 1978, when Adventure-Life Reformed Church was organized, she became its secretary and served there for 26 years before retirement.
funFacts: She and Larry Lourens were charter members of Adventure-Life Reformed Church.
Her obituary notes long volunteer service at Caring Hands Food Pantry in Altoona.
Her hobbies included reading, scrapbooking, embroidering, camping, and traveling.
records: Hamilton's Funeral Home obituary
The Oskaloosa Herald obituary

citations.label: Hamilton's Funeral Home obituary: Joyce Kathryn Lourens
The Oskaloosa Herald obituary: Joyce Kathryn Lourens

bio: Epp R. Roorda was born near Pella, Iowa, to John E. and Adriana Van Maaren Roorda and married Cornelia Vander Wilt in 1897. His Pella Chronicle obituary says he and Cornelia had seven children, and that he was survived by a very large family network of grandchildren and great-grandchildren. He spent his last decade at the Pella Rest Home and died at Pella Community Hospital at age 91.
funFacts: He was a member of First Reformed Church in Pella for about 40 years.
His obituary says he served on the church consistory for a number of years.
At his death he was survived by seven children, 19 grandchildren, 3 step-grandchildren, and 44 great-grandchildren.
records: Pella Chronicle obituary transcribed on Find a Grave

citations.label: Find a Grave memorial with Pella Chronicle obituary: Epp Robert Roorda

bio: Cornelia Vander Wilt Roorda was born north of Pella, daughter of John Vander Wilt and Gora/Govertje Den Burger. She married Epp R. Roorda on 10 February 1897, and their obituary record names seven living children at her death in 1949. She lived at 1116 Marion Street in Pella at the time of her death.
funFacts: Her obituary says she had 19 grandchildren and 4 great-grandchildren by 1949.
She had a twin sister, Sarah, and the obituary names one brother and nine sisters who predeceased her.
She was described as a faithful First Reformed Church member who attended worship until lingering illness prevented it.
records: Pella Chronicle obituary transcribed on Find a Grave

citations.label: Find a Grave memorial with Pella Chronicle obituary: Cornelia Vander Wilt Roorda

bio: Ruurd, also called John, Epkes Roorda was the youngest child of Epke Alberts Roorda and Froukje Wiegers van der Lune. The family history in IAGenWeb's translation of Greetings from America says he came to America with his parents in 1857 and later married Adrianna Jane van Maren in Pella in 1868. He farmed in the Durham neighborhood and also peddled housewares, eggs, and vegetables door to door.
funFacts: He celebrated his 11th birthday aboard ship on the family's 1857 journey to America.
He and Adrianna Jane van Maren had 12 children: 10 sons and 2 daughters.
His siblings' immigration was staggered: three preceded the parents to America, four traveled with the parents, and two stayed in the Netherlands.
immigration: Immigrated from Dantumawoude, Friesland, to Pella, Iowa, in 1857 with his parents and siblings Hendrik, Gerrit, and Hinke.
records: IAGenWeb translation of Greetings from America, Chapter 9

citations.label: IAGenWeb: Greetings from America, Chapter 9, Family of Epke Alberts Roorda

bio: Adrianna Jane van Maren married Ruurd (John) Epkes Roorda on 7 October 1868 in Pella. The family history identifies her as the daughter of Maren and Antje van der Linden and says she was born on 23 September 1849, possibly in Friesland. She and John raised a large Roorda family in the Pella area.
funFacts: The family history lists 12 children for Adrianna and John: Henry, John, Epp, Wieger, Luke, Anna, Andrew, Albert, Izaak, Anne, Gerrit, and Janie.
Her husband's work combined farming near Durham with peddling housewares, eggs, and vegetables door to door.
records: IAGenWeb translation of Greetings from America, Chapter 9

citations.label: IAGenWeb: Greetings from America, Chapter 9, Family of Epke Alberts Roorda

bio: Jan Vander Wilt appears to match the younger Jan listed in the van der Wilt household on the 1847 Pieter Floris passenger list transcribed in the WikiTree Pella immigrant project. The household included Gerrit van der Wilt, Sophia Spruit, several older children with farmer occupations, and Jan, age 15, also listed as a farmer. This places him among the Dutch immigrant families who followed the early Pella settlement stream.
funFacts: The same passenger-list household included multiple working-age van der Wilt sons identified as farmers.
His daughter Cornelia Vander Wilt Roorda's obituary says Cornelia was born north of Pella and identifies her parents as John Vander Wilt and Gora/Govertje Den Burger.
immigration: Likely immigrated in 1847 on the Pieter Floris as part of a van der Wilt household from the Netherlands to Baltimore, then onward to the Pella, Iowa settlement.
records: WikiTree Pella immigrant passenger-list transcription
Pella Chronicle obituary for daughter Cornelia

bio: Govertje Den Burger appears in the den Burger household on the 1847 Maasstroom passenger list transcribed for the WikiTree Pella immigrant project. She was listed as age 11 in the household of Jan den Burger, alongside Mantje and several older children. In Iowa records she is represented in her daughter Cornelia Vander Wilt Roorda's obituary as Gora/Govertje Den Burger, wife of John Vander Wilt.
funFacts: She was still a child when the den Burger family crossed the Atlantic in 1847.
Her daughter Cornelia's obituary shows the family settled north of Pella and became tied into the Roorda line through Cornelia's marriage to Epp R. Roorda.
immigration: Listed as an 11-year-old in the den Burger household on the 1847 Maasstroom passenger list associated with the Dutch migration to Pella, Iowa.
records: WikiTree Pella immigrant passenger-list transcription
Pella Chronicle obituary for daughter Cornelia

bio: Klaas Hendrik de Fockert belongs to the Herwijnen De Fockert/Hackert line documented in the Pella Community Memory Database's thirty-one-page Dutch register of the family. A page of that register names Klaas Hendrik de Fockert, born at Herwijnen on 20 December 1840, and records his marriage at Herwijnen on 15 March 1867 to Cornelia Hakkert/Rakkert. The same register continues with their children, including Pieter de Fockert, born at Herwijnen on 5 February 1880.
funFacts: The Pella Community Memory Database describes the De Fockert register as covering the family in Herwijnen from 1601 to 1942 and including a family crest.
PCMD metadata for a related Hackert family file notes that the surname Hackert was previously known as DeFockert.
records: Pella Community Memory Database: Register of the Family De Fockert in Herwijnen

citations.label: PCMD: Register of the Family De Fockert in Herwijnen
PCMD: De Fockert register, page naming Klaas Hendrik and Cornelia
PCMD search result: Hackert surname formerly DeFockert

bio: Cornelia Hakkert/Rakkert is named in the Herwijnen De Fockert family register as the wife of Klaas Hendrik de Fockert. The register page says Klaas Hendrik married her at Herwijnen on 15 March 1867 and identifies her as the daughter of Klaas and Maria van Zee, born at Herwijnen on 15 October 1845. Their children are continued in the register, tying the Dutch De Fockert/Hackert family to the later Pella Hackert line.
funFacts: Her son Pieter de Fockert, born 5 February 1880 at Herwijnen, matches the Peter Hackert later found in the Pella branch.
The source preserves the older Dutch family form De Fockert beside the later Hackert spelling.
records: Pella Community Memory Database: Register of the Family De Fockert in Herwijnen

citations.label: PCMD: De Fockert register, page naming Klaas Hendrik and Cornelia
PCMD: De Fockert register, children of Klaas Hendrik and Cornelia

bio: Peter Hackert matches Pieter de Fockert in the Herwijnen De Fockert register: the page for children of Klaas Hendrik de Fockert and Cornelia Rakkert lists Pieter de Fockert born at Herwijnen on 5 February 1880. In Pella records, his son Henry Hackert's obituary identifies Henry as the son of Peter and Johanna (De Wild) Hackert and says Henry was born in Pella in 1916. This links Peter's Dutch birth family with the Hackert household south of Pella.
funFacts: His son Henry lived most of his life in the same home south of Pella and worked on the farm all his life, according to Henry's obituary.
Henry Hackert later combined farming with 18 years as a rural mail carrier for the United States Postal Service.
records: Pella Community Memory Database De Fockert register
IAGenWeb obituary for son Henry Hackert

citations.label: PCMD: De Fockert register, children of Klaas Hendrik and Cornelia
IAGenWeb Marion County obituary: Henry Hackert

bio: Johanna DeWild appears in Pella records as Johanna (De Wild) Hackert, wife of Peter Hackert and mother of Henry Hackert. Henry's obituary says he was born in Pella on 23 February 1916, son of Peter and Johanna (De Wild) Hackert, and that he lived most of his life in the same home south of Pella. Through daughter Wilhelmina Hackert Roorda, Johanna's line connects directly into the Roorda/Lourens branch.
funFacts: Henry Hackert's obituary names his sisters Cornelia Schakel and Wilhelmina Roorda and brother Clarence Hackert, confirming several children of Peter and Johanna.
The obituary places the family in the Third Reformed Church community in Pella through Henry's funeral and membership.
records: IAGenWeb obituary for son Henry Hackert

citations.label: IAGenWeb Marion County obituary: Henry Hackert

bio: Epke Alberts Roorda was born at Rinsumageest in Friesland and married Froukje Wiegers van der Lune at Ferwerd in 1822. The family history says he was a painter and glassmaker, later kept a boarding house, and resumed painting after settling in Marion County, Iowa. He and Froukje emigrated from Dantumawoude to Pella in 1857 with four children, while three children had already gone to America and two remained in the Netherlands.
funFacts: Epke painted landscapes on glass in his spare time after arriving in America.
He and Froukje had 11 children, two of whom died young in Dantumawoude.
According to the History of Pella tradition quoted by IAGenWeb, the couple brought plum trees, rhubarb, roses, and other plants to America.
Their son Hendrik served in Company H, 17th Iowa Volunteers, and died during the Civil War at Corinth, Mississippi.
immigration: Left Dantumawoude for Pella, Iowa, in 1857 with wife Froukje and children Hendrik, Gerrit, Hinke, and Ruurd/John.
records: IAGenWeb translation of Greetings from America, Chapter 9
Find a Grave memorial biography

bio: Froukje Wiegers van der Lune was born at Hallum, Ferwerderadeel, daughter of Wieger Pieters van der Lune and Antje Gerrits Kooi. She married Epke Alberts Roorda at Ferwerd in 1822 and was matriarch of a large Roorda family that moved from Friesland to Pella, Iowa. The family history emphasizes that she and Epke were garden lovers as well as immigrants, carrying plants and family traditions into the Dutch community at Pella.
funFacts: The couple had 11 children, and two died young before the family emigrated.
Their family reportedly brought plum trees, rhubarb, roses, and other plants to America.
A PCMD photograph identifies Mr. and Mrs. Epke Alberts Roorda and notes their 30 May 1822 marriage.
immigration: Immigrated from Friesland to Pella, Iowa, in 1857 with Epke and four of their children; other children had already preceded them to America.
records: IAGenWeb translation of Greetings from America, Chapter 9
Pella Community Memory Database photograph

bio: Jan van Maren appears in the Pieter Floris passenger-list transcription as Jan van Maren, age 37, farmer, traveling with Areke nee van der Linden and children Hendrik, Lucas, and Adriana. The family belonged to the 1847 Dutch immigrant stream connected with the founding generation of Pella, Iowa. His daughter Adrianna Jane van Maren later married Ruurd/John Epkes Roorda, joining the van Maren and Roorda lines in Marion County.
funFacts: The Pieter Floris list places Jan's household among many Dutch farm families bound for the Pella settlement.
The IAGenWeb Roorda family history names Adrianna Jane van Maren as John Roorda's wife and connects her to the van Maren/van der Linden family.
immigration: Listed as a 37-year-old farmer on the 1847 Pieter Floris passenger list for Dutch immigrants who settled in Pella, Iowa.
records: WikiTree Pella immigrant passenger-list transcription
IAGenWeb Roorda family history

bio: Areke van der Linden appears in the 1847 Pieter Floris passenger-list transcription as Areke, nee van der Linden, age 41, housewife, traveling with Jan van Maren and children Hendrik, Lucas, and Adriana. This places her in the Dutch migration stream that founded and populated Pella, Iowa. Her van Maren household later connected to the Roorda line through Adrianna Jane van Maren's marriage to Ruurd/John Epkes Roorda.
funFacts: The passenger-list entry preserves her maiden name directly as van der Linden.
Her household traveled with many other Dutch families whose occupations were heavily agricultural, reflecting the farming character of the early Pella colony.
immigration: Listed as age 41 in Jan van Maren's household on the 1847 Pieter Floris passenger list associated with the Dutch settlement at Pella, Iowa.
records: WikiTree Pella immigrant passenger-list transcription

citations.label: WikiTree: Dutch immigrants who settled in Pella, Pieter Floris passenger list

bio: Jan den Burger appears in the Maasstroom passenger-list transcription as Jan, age 55, traveling with Mantje and children including Cornelis, Arie, Joost, Patentje, Govertje, and Jan. The older sons Cornelis, Arie, and Joost are listed as farmers, suggesting the family joined the agrarian Dutch settler stream to Iowa. His daughter Govertje/Grace later married into the Vander Wilt family and became an ancestor of Cornelia Vander Wilt Roorda.
funFacts: The den Burger family was part of the 1847 Dutch immigrant movement associated with Hendrik Pieter Scholte's Pella settlement.
Several older children in the passenger-list household were already working-age farmers.
immigration: Listed at age 55 in the den Burger household on the 1847 Maasstroom passenger list connected with Dutch immigrants to Pella, Iowa.
records: WikiTree Pella immigrant passenger-list transcription
